data.title: Fed's Waller backs 25-bp rate cut at upcoming meeting, citing job-market weakness
data.description:
Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller said Thursday the U.S. central bank should lower interest rates by another quarter-point at the next policy meeting late in the month, reiterating his view that the labor market and inflation (excluding "temporary tariff effects") both are softening.

Waller sees the neutral rate -- the rate that neither hinders nor stimulates economic activity -- some 100 to 125 bps lower than it the current 4.0%-4.25% federal funds rate target range, which was already reduced by 25 bps in September for the first time this year.

At the upcoming Oct. 28-29 Federal Open Market Committee gathering, he said, monetary policy should move toward a more neutral position. From there, he said the appropriate course will depend on how the tension between solid economic growth and a cooling labor market plays out, as well as on the outlook for inflation.

But, looking further out, he cautioned uncertainty stemming from "a conflict right now between data showing solid growth in economic activity and data showing a softening labor market. So, something's gotta give—either economic growth softens to match a soft labor market, or the labor market rebounds to match stronger economic growth," he said in remarks prepared for delivery at the Council on Foreign Relations in New York.

"I believe that how that process plays out in the coming months will have a significant impact on the path of monetary policy," he said, especially as the ongoing government shutdown delays the release of key economic data for policymakers to assess.

With the lack of public data, the Fed governor said he spoke to business contacts to help him shape his economic outlook. "Employers indicate to me that there was some further softening of the labor market last month, while retailers report continued solid spending, with a bit of caution from lower-income households," he said.

This is a developing story. Check back for updates.

data.title: Bank of Montreal to sell 138 branches to First Citizens to optimize US presence
data.description:
Bank of Montreal (NYSE:BMO) agreed to sell 138 BMO branches to First Citizens Bank & Trust (NASDAQ:FCNCA) (OTCPK:FCNCB) in a drive to optimize its U.S. branch network and reinvest in markets with strong long-term growth potential, the company said on Thursday.

BMO is selling branches in areas including North Dakota, South Dakota, Wyoming, Nebraska, Kansas, Missouri, Oklahoma, and Idaho, but plans to open 150 new branches over the next five years, mostly concentrated in California.

First Citizens Bank will assume ~$5.7B in deposit liabilities and acquire ~$1.1B in loans. As a result of the transaction, BMO (NYSE:BMO) will record a charge related to goodwill of ~US$75M (C$104M), before and after-tax, in Q4 2025. On closing, the bank expects to record a tax expense of US$85M (C$117M). The impact to BMO's CET1 ratio isn't expected to be material.

The deal is expected to close in mid-2026. Financial terms of the transaction weren't disclosed.

For First Citizens (NASDAQ:FCNCA), the acquisition pushes its business into new markets. "This deposit franchise is solid, and we look forward to serving individuals and business clients in these areas," First Citizens Bank Chairman and CEO Frank B. Holding, Jr. said. "Additionally, the net deposit position is expected to enable us to further enhance our liquidity position and provide additional flexibility to support our strategic initiatives."

Bank of Montreal (BMO) stock edged up 0.1% in early Thursday trading.  First Citizens Bancshares (NASDAQ:FCNCA) class A shares slipped 0.5%.

data.title: Wall Street gains as traders assess more corporate earnings; U.S. government shutdown drags on
data.description:
The stock market advanced on Thursday as investors assessed more earnings reports, and digested a few speeches from the Federal Reserve members.

S&P 500 (SP500) +0.3%, the Dow (DJI) +0.3%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.05%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.52%.

U.S. stocks ended mixed after a choppy session on Wednesday, with the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) gaining ground as upbeat earnings reports eclipsed U.S.-China trade tension worries.

"Markets recovered some ground yesterday as strong earnings and more positive signals on the U.S.-China relationship helped to boost investor optimism," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"This backdrop led to a topsy-turvy session, with a positive mood dominating overall as the S&P 500 closed 0.40% higher, though it had been up as much as 1.20% shortly after Scott Bessent’s comments. The trade-exposed areas saw a clear outperformance, seemingly reflecting investors’ growing confidence that the full 100% tariffs will ultimately be avoided," Allen said.

Treasury Secretary Bessent had reportedly said that President Donald Trump "is a go" for a meeting with Xi Jinping and that he was positive about the ongoing discussions with China. 

The U.S. government shutdown is now entering its third week. 

Over to the economic calendar for the day. There are quite a few Fed speakers lined up, including Richmond Fed President Thomas Barkin, Fed Board Governor Christopher Waller, Federal Governor Stephen Miran, and Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari, among others.

The Fed balance sheet is due later in the day.

data.title: HCI Group's Exzeo launches IPO
data.description:
HCI Group (NYSE:HCI) on Thursday announced that its insurance technology unit, Exzeo, has launched an initial public offering for its common stock.

Exzeo has filed a registration statement on Form S-1 with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

The unit is offering 8M shares of common stock. The IPO price is currently expected to be between $20.00 and $22.00 per share.

Exzeo has applied to list its common stock on the NYSE under the ticker symbol XZO.

HCI will retain majority ownership of the unit.

Additionally, the underwriters are intended to be granted a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 1.2M shares at the IPO price.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson succeeds in late-stage multiple myeloma trial
data.description:
• Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) has announced that its late-stage trial for Tecvayli (teclistamab), used in combination with Darzalex Faspro, achieved its main goal in treating relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma.
  • This combination was compared to the investigator's choice of either Darzalex Faspro with pomalidomide and dexamethasone or Darzalex Faspro with bortezomib and dexamethasone.
  • Additionally, the secondary endpoint concerning overall survival showed statistically significant results during the first interim analysis.
  • The company said that the safety profile of the treatment combination was consistent with the known safety profiles of each monotherapy.

data.title: DeFi Development acquires 86,307 SOL
data.description:
• DeFi Development (NASDAQ:DFDV) has acquired 86,307 Solana (SOL) at an average price of $110.91 per SOL, the company said on Thursday.
  • This purchase represents a 4.7% increase, bringing DeFi Dev Corp.’s aggregate SOL and SOL-equivalent position to 2,195,926 SOL, valued at ~$426 million.
  • DFDV +2.19% premarket to $15.37.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bearish sentiment climbs as investor optimism fades; shows latest AAII survey
data.description:
Bearish sentiment among individual investors increased to 46.1% for the week ending October 15, 2025, according to the latest survey from the American Association of Individual Investors (AAII). This marks a rise from 35.6% in the prior week, reflecting growing caution among market participants.

Bullish sentiment declined to 33.7% from 45.9% a week earlier, indicating a pullback in investor optimism despite recent equity market strength. 

Investor sentiment turned cautious during the week as renewed U.S.–China trade tensions, the ongoing U.S. government shutdown and mixed global data kept markets volatile. Wall Street saw uneven trading, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq posting small gains helped by earnings, while European indexes hovered near flat amid political uncertainty.

Gold climbed above $4,000 and silver neared record levels as investors sought safety. In Asia, markets were mixed, with Australia hitting record highs on rate-cut hopes and China’s consumer prices falling further.

The latest results show investors turning more defensive, with bullish sentiment dropping sharply from last week and bearish views rising to their highest level this month.

Neutral sentiment, which reflects expectations for little change in stock prices over the next six months, edged up to 20.3% from 18.5% in the previous week. This suggests that more investors are adopting a cautious, wait-and-see approach amid recent market volatility.

The survey, conducted by the AAII since 1987, continues to provide key insights into retail investor sentiment and how individual investors are positioning themselves for market conditions in the near term.

S&P 500 Tracking Funds: (MUTF:FXAIX), (MUTF:VFIAX), (MUTF:VFFSX), (MUTF:SWPPX), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS) and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Diversified Energy commits $70M to launch West Virginia well plugging fund
data.description:
Diversified Energy (NYSE:DEC) said Thursday it is teaming up with the state of West Virginia to establish the first financial assurance fund in the U.S. dedicated to retiring oil and gas wells, representing a $70 million commitment over an initial 20-year period in which the company agrees to retire at least 1,500 wells.

Diversified Energy (NYSE:DEC) said the Mountain State Plugging Fund ensures that its oil and natural gas wells in West Virginia will be safely retired at no cost to taxpayers, setting a new responsible stewardship standard for aging energy infrastructure across the U.S.

The company said the fund's $70 million principal will be guaranteed by OneNexus, an insurance provider of financial assurance for asset retirement obligations, with the state of West Virginia designated as a third-party beneficiary, ensuring all the company's wells in the state are safely plugged and retired.

"Together, West Virginia and Diversified Energy are setting a model for how energy-producing states can safeguard the environment while ensuring long-term economic growth," the company said.

data.title: Nebius Group introduces Nebius AI cloud 3.0 Aether
data.description:
• Nebius Group (NASDAQ:NBIS) announced on Thursday that it has introduced its latest AI cloud - Nebius AI Cloud 3.0 “Aether” - delivering enterprise-grade features that give organizations the trust, control, and simplicity they need to run their most critical AI workloads in production and at scale.
  • The company said its latest AI cloud will provide enterprises independently validated security certifications, in addition to fine-grained governance with native observability, granular IAM, and full infrastructure visibility, as well as streamlined developer workflows that accelerate deployment.
  • “Aether proves that enterprise control and builder productivity can coexist on the same platform. With comprehensive security and governance features, and a constant focus on the developer experience, we're delivering the infrastructure enterprises need to move from AI experimentation to business-critical production,” said Roman Chernin, co-founder and Chief Business Officer of Nebius.
  • Last month, Nebius inked an agreement with Microsoft (MSFT) to provide artificial intelligence infrastructure for $17.4B over the next five years.
  • NBIS +2.54% premarket to $129.03.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: B. Riley starts coverage of Bitmine, SharpLink, FG Nexus, Kindly MD, Sequans with Buy ratings
data.description:
B. Riley Securities initiated coverage of five digital asset treasury companies - Bitmine Immersion (NYSE:BMNR), SharpLink Gaming (NASDAQ:SBET), FG Nexus (NASDAQ:FGNX), Kindly MD (NASDAQ:NAKA), Sequans Communications (NYSE:SQNS) - with a Buy rating.

"DATCOs (digital asset treasury companies) exploit market opportunities through capital allocation," said B. Riley analyst Fedor Shabalin in a research note.

"When their stock prices exceed the value of their crypto holdings (trading at a premium to NAV), they issue new shares to buy more crypto, thus increasing assets per share. When trading below NAV (at a discount), they buy back shares to boost NAV/share. This strategy capitalizes on valuation gaps while offering investors regulated stock market access to digital assets," said the note.

"We believe bullish crypto investors should consider DATCOs because quality companies in this space offer leveraged exposure to crypto returns through their active treasury management strategies," said Shabalin.

The five DATCO stocks were trading higher Thursday pre-market. BMNR was +2.19% to $54.98, SBET +4.69% to $15.86, FGNX +1.05% to $3.86, NAKA +0.01% to $0.80, and SQNS +0.50% to $8.01.

Here is a look at the average Wall Street analysts, Seeking Alpha authors, and Quant ratings on the stocks:

Bitmine Immersion

  • Sell-side analysts - Strong Buy
  • SA authors - Buy
  • Quant - Hold

SharpLink Gaming

  • Sell-side analysts - Strong Buy
  • SA authors - Hold
  • Quant - Hold

FG Nexus

  • Sell-side analysts - Strong Buy
  • SA authors - Not Covered
  • Quant - Hold

Kindly MD

  • Sell-side analysts - Strong Buy
  • SA authors - Sell
  • Quant - Strong Sell

Sequans Communications

  • Sell-side analysts - Strong Buy
  • SA authors - Hold
  • Quant - Sell
